recipes.title: Ninja Creami Apple Cinnamon Pie Ice Cream
instructions: Add cottage cheese, sweetened condensed milk, apples, half and half, brown sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, and sea salt to a blender.
Blend until completely smooth. This matters.
Pour the mixture into a 24 oz Deluxe Ninja Creami pint.
Freeze on a flat surface for at least 24 hours.
Spin on Ice Cream. If crumbly, respin.
Add  graham crackers using the Mix-In cycle.
recipes.prepNotes: This apple cinnamon pie Ninja Creami ice cream is rich, cozy, and packed with real pie flavor. Made with half and half, cottage cheese, and brown sugar, it spins up creamy with classic graham cracker crunch.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Chai Tea Latte Ice Cream
instructions: Steep the tea: Add chai tea bags to 1/2 cup hot water. Steep 5-7 minutes until dark and concentrated. Remove tea bags and let cool to room temperature.
Blend the base: Add cooled tea, half and half, cottage cheese, sweetened condensed milk, vanilla extract, salt, and cinnamon to a blender. Blend on high for 30 seconds until completely smooth.
Freeze: Pour into Ninja Creami pint container (don't exceed max fill line). Freeze on a flat surface for 24 hours.
Spin: Remove pint from freezer. Take off lid and place in outer bowl. Attach paddle lid and select **Ice Cream**.
Check texture: If crumbly or powdery (normal for tea-based ice cream), make a small well in the center and add 1-2 tablespoons half and half or milk. Select **Respin**.
Serve: Scoop immediately for soft-serve texture, or freeze 20-30 minutes for firmer scoops.
recipes.prepNotes: This chai tea latte ice cream is creamy, warmly spiced, and made with real chai tea bags - no pudding mix or protein powder needed.

recipes.title: Ninja CREAMi Plain Tart Frozen Yogurt
instructions: Blend – In a blender, mix Greek yogurt, honey, and lemon juice. Blend until smooth.
Freeze – Pour into a Ninja CREAMi pint container and freeze for at least 24 hours.
Spin – Place in the Ninja CREAMi machine and select Lite Ice Cream or Yogurt
Check texture – If crumbly, add 1-2 tbsp milk and re-spin.
Enjoy! Serve as is or add your favorite mix-ins.
recipes.prepNotes: Love frozen yogurt but want a healthier homemade version? This Ninja CREAMi Plain Tart Frozen Yogurt is creamy, tangy, and so easy to make!

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Classic Coffee Ice Cream (High Protein)
instructions: Blend half and half, espresso, cottage cheese, condensed milk, and vanilla until smooth
Freeze flat in a Ninja Creami pint container for 24 hours
Spin using “Lite Ice Cream”  function
Respin if crumbly—add 1-2 tbsp milk if needed
Serve & Enjoy—grab a spoon and dig in!
recipes.prepNotes: This high-protein Ninja Creami coffee ice cream is creamy, bold, and made with real espresso flavor. Cottage cheese adds protein and smooth texture — no gums, no pudding mix, no protein powder.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Maple Cinnamon Toast Crunch Ice Cream
ingredients: ⅔ cup Cinnamon Toast Crunch cereal
1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste or extract
instructions: Add half and half, condensed milk, cottage cheese, maple syrup, vanilla, cinnamon powder, and salt to a blender. Blend until totally smooth.
Pour into a Deluxe pint up to the max fill line.
Freeze for 24 hours.
Spin on Ice Cream cycle.
If crumbly, Respin. Add 1–2 Tbsp half and half if needed.
Create a center tunnel, add Cinnamon Toast Crunch, and Mix-In.
Serve immediately or freeze briefly for a firmer scoop.
recipes.prepNotes: A creamy, breakfast-inspired maple ice cream packed with cinnamon cereal crunch. Made with simple ingredients - no gums, no protein powder - and frozen to Ninja Creami perfection.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Shamrock Ice Cream
ingredients: ½ teaspoon Mint chocolate chip flavor I used Lorann Oils Mint Chocolate Chip
1 teaspoon Vanilla extract or vanilla bean paste
instructions: Blend Ingredients – Combine everything in a blender until smooth.
Freeze for 24 Hours – Pour into a Ninja Creami pint and freeze on a level surface.
Spin on Ice Cream Mode – Let the Ninja Creami do its magic!
Respin if Needed – If it’s crumbly, respin for the perfect texture.
Serve and Enjoy – Scoop it up and enjoy a creamy, minty treat!
recipes.prepNotes: This Ninja Creami Shamrock Ice Cream is minty, creamy, and smooth with real ingredients — no pudding mix, no gums, and perfect for St. Patrick’s Day.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Mint Cookies and Cream Ice Cream
ingredients: ¼ teaspoon Mint chocolate chip flavor I used Lorann Oils Mint Chocolate Chip (add more if you want)
Crushed oreo cookies add more if you want
instructions: Blend: Add all the ingredients to a blender or food processor and blend until smooth.
Freeze: Pour the mixture into a Ninja Creami pint container. Freeze for 24 hours on a level surface.
Spin: Remove the lid and spin using the "Ice Cream" mode on your Ninja Creami.
Respin (if needed): If the texture is crumbly, add 1–2 tablespoons of milk and hit the "Respin" button.
Add mix-ins: If you didn’t already mix in crushed Oreos, now’s a great time. Fold them in and enjoy!
recipes.prepNotes: This Ninja Creami mint cookies and cream ice cream is creamy, cool, and loaded with Oreo chunks. No pudding mix, no gums, just a reliable base that spins smooth every time.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Mint Chocolate Chip Ice Cream
ingredients: ½ teaspoon Mint chocolate chip flavor (I used Lorann Oils Mint Chocolate Chip)
1 teaspoon Vanilla extract or vanilla bean paste
2 tablespoon Chocolate chips or mint chocolate chips (chopped)
instructions: In a blender, combine half and half, sweetened condensed milk, cottage cheese, vanilla, and mint flavoring. Blend until smooth and creamy.
Pour the mixture into a Ninja Creami pint container. Freeze for at least 24 hours.
Once frozen, place the container in the Ninja Creami machine. Select the Ice Cream setting and let it spin.
If too crumbly, add a splash of milk and respin.
Stir in chocolate chips or use Mix in function and enjoy!
Serve & Enjoy – Scoop into a bowl and dig in!
recipes.prepNotes: If you love mint chocolate chip ice cream but don’t want it to taste like you’re eating toothpaste, you’re in the right place! This homemade Ninja Creami mint chocolate chip ice cream is perfectly balanced—cool, creamy, and loaded with just the right amount of chocolatey goodness.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Hot Honey Ice Cream
ingredients: 1 teaspoon Vanilla Extract or vanilla bean paste
instructions: Blend the ingredients: In a blender, combine half-and-half, hot honey, cottage cheese, vanilla, and salt. Blend until smooth.
Freeze: Pour the mixture into a Ninja CREAMi pint container. Freeze for 12-24 hours.
Spin in Ninja CREAMi: Insert the pint into the Ninja CREAMi Deluxe and select the “Ice Cream” setting.
Re-spin if needed: If the texture is crumbly, add 1-2 tbsp of milk and re-spin until creamy.
Enjoy! Serve plain or with fun toppings!
recipes.prepNotes: Sweet, creamy, and with a spicy kick—this Hot Honey Ice Cream made in the Ninja Creami is the ultimate sweet-heat treat! With just the right balance of rich dairy and a drizzle of spicy honey, this easy recipe will keep you coming back for more.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Cookies and Cream Ice Cream
instructions: Add the milk, heavy cream, sweetened condensed milk, vanilla extract, and salt directly to the Ninja Creami pint container. Use a small whisk, milk frother, or spoon to mix until fully combined and smooth.
Freeze on a level surface for 24 hours until completely solid.
Remove the pint from the freezer and place it into the Ninja Creami outer bowl.
Process using the Ice Cream setting.
If the texture looks crumbly or powdery, add 1 tablespoon milk and run Respin once.
Create a hole in the center of the ice cream, add the broken Oreo cookies, and run the Mix-In cycle.
Scoop, serve, and try not to eat it straight from the pint standing at the counter.
recipes.prepNotes: This Ninja Creami Cookies and Cream Ice Cream is rich, creamy, and packed with crunchy Oreo cookie pieces in every bite. No cream cheese, no pudding mix, and no weird extras—just a smooth homemade vanilla base with classic cookies and cream flavor. It tastes like the real deal and disappears fast in my house.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Peanut Butter Cup Ice Cream
instructions: Blend the base. Add the milk, heavy cream, sweetened condensed milk, creamy peanut butter, and salt to a blender. Blend until completely smooth, about 45 to 60 seconds, making sure the peanut butter is fully incorporated with no streaks.
Pour and freeze. Pour the mixture into your Ninja Creami pint container, making sure it does not go past the max fill line. Cover with the lid and freeze for 24 hours, or until completely solid.

Thaw slightly, then spin. Remove the pint from the freezer and let it sit at room temperature for about 5 minutes. Process on the Ice Cream setting. If it looks crumbly after the first spin, run a Re-Spin.
Add the mix-ins. Make a hole in the center of the ice cream with a spoon. Chop the mini peanut butter cups and add them to the center. Process on the Mix-In setting.
Serve. Scoop and serve right away.
recipes.prepNotes: This Ninja Creami peanut butter cup ice cream is creamy, rich, and packed with peanut butter flavor in every bite. The base comes together in minutes with milk, cream, sweetened condensed milk, and real peanut butter, then gets finished with chopped mini peanut butter cups for that classic candy-style mix-in.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Black Sesame Ice Cream
instructions: Whisk the whole milk, heavy cream, sugar, black sesame paste, vanilla extract, and salt in a bowl or large measuring cup until the sugar dissolves and the sesame paste is fully blended. The mixture should look smooth and evenly dark gray.
Pour the mixture into a Ninja Creami pint container, making sure it stays below the max fill line.
Cover with the lid and freeze on a flat, level surface for at least 24 hours.
Remove the pint from the freezer and let it sit on the counter for 5 minutes before processing.
Place the pint into the Ninja Creami machine and run the Ice Cream cycle.
Check the texture after the first spin. If it looks crumbly, powdery, or uneven, run the Re-spin cycle. Add 1 tablespoon of milk before respinning if needed.
Serve right away for a softer texture, or freeze for 30 minutes if you want firmer, more classic ice cream scoops.
recipes.prepNotes: This Ninja Creami black sesame ice cream is rich, creamy, nutty, and lightly toasty with a beautiful deep gray color. It uses a simple no-cook base and black sesame paste for smooth texture and bold flavor.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Blackberry Cheesecake Ice Cream
instructions: Blend it up: Blend all ingredients in a blender or food processor until smooth.
Freeze: Pour the mixture into a Ninja Creami Deluxe pint and freeze on a level surface for at least 24 hours.
Spin: Use the “Lite Ice Cream” or “Gelato” mode to spin.
Re-spin: If crumbly, add 1–2 tbsp milk and re-spin until creamy.
Serve & enjoy: Scoop and devour!
recipes.prepNotes: This Ninja Creami Blackberry Cheesecake Ice Cream is creamy, tangy, and bursting with juicy berries. If you love cheesecake and blackberries, you’re about to meet your new favorite frozen treat!

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Watermelon Ice Cream (2 ingredients)
instructions: Blend watermelon and sweetened condensed milk until smooth.
Pour into a Ninja Creami Deluxe pint container.
Freeze on a level surface for 24 hours.
Spin using “Lite Ice Cream” mode.
Re-spin if crumbly.
Scoop and enjoy your creamy watermelon treat!
recipes.prepNotes: This 2-Ingredient Watermelon Ice Cream Is Summer in a Pint. When it’s blazing hot out, nothing hits like a cold, creamy scoop of watermelon ice cream. This easy Ninja Creami recipe only needs two ingredients—but tastes like a store-bought favorite.

recipes.title: Ninja CREAMi Sugar Free Blackberry Sorbet
ingredients: 2 cups Fresh blackberries (You can use frozen ones too!)
3 tablespoon monk fruit (adjust according to your taste)
instructions: Blend the Ingredients – In a blender or food processor, puree blackberries, monk fruit, water, and lemon juice until smooth.
Strain If Needed – If you want a seed-free sorbet, strain the mixture.
Freeze in a Pint Container – Pour into a Ninja CREAMi pint container and freeze for 24 hours.
Spin to Perfection – Place in the Ninja CREAMi machine, select the sorbet setting, and spin.
Respin If Needed – If it’s too crumbly, add a splash of water and spin again.
Enjoy! – Scoop and serve immediately!
recipes.prepNotes: If you love fresh berries and refreshing frozen treats, this Ninja CREAMi sugar free blackberry sorbet is a must-try! It’s simple, delicious, and made with just a few ingredients.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Creamy Strawberry Sorbet
instructions: Blend the Ingredients: Start by blending strawberries, sugar, and water in a blender or food processor until smooth.

Heat the Mixture: Gently heat the blended mixture in a saucepan. Warm it without boiling. Stir in a slurry made from tapioca starch or cornstarch mixed with water, stirring continuously until slightly thickened.
Freeze Overnight: Pour your mixture into a Ninja Creami pint container. Freeze on a level surface for 24 hours for best results.
Spin to Creamy Perfection: Remove your pint from the freezer and spin using the sorbet setting on your Ninja Creami machine.
Respin if Needed: If it's not creamy enough, simply respin once more.
Serve and Enjoy: Scoop your creamy strawberry sorbet and enjoy immediately!
recipes.prepNotes: Forget everything you know about classic sorbet—this creamy  strawberry sorbet made in your Ninja Creami takes it to a whole new level! Just one taste, and you'll never settle for ordinary sorbet again.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Blueberry Cheesecake Ice Cream
instructions: Macerate – Sprinkle sugar over the blueberries and let them sit for about 10 minutes to release their natural juices and enhance the flavor.
Blend – Add the macerated blueberries and the remaining ingredients to a blender or food processor and blend until smooth.
Freeze – Pour into a Ninja Creami pint container, cover, and freeze for 24 hours on a level surface.
Spin – Place in the Ninja Creami and use the Ice Cream mode to churn.
Respin – If the texture is crumbly, respin until it’s smooth and creamy.
Serve & enjoy – Scoop into a bowl, add toppings, and dig in!
recipes.prepNotes: If you love blueberries and cheesecake, this Ninja Creami Blueberry Cheesecake Ice Cream is for you! It’s smooth, creamy, and packed with sweet blueberry flavor—all made easily in your Ninja Creami.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Cherry Ice Cream Recpe
instructions: Blend: Add all ingredients to a blender or food processor. Blend until completely smooth.
Freeze: Pour the mixture into a Ninja Creami pint container. Level the top, cover, and freeze for 24 hours on a flat surface.
Spin: Remove the lid and insert the pint into the Ninja Creami Deluxe machine. Select the Ice Cream function.
Respin (If Needed): If the texture is crumbly, add 1–2 tablespoons of milk and use the Respin function.
Serve: Scoop and enjoy immediately or transfer to a container for later.
recipes.prepNotes: This is the cherry ice cream I kept wishing existed—creamy, and actually full of real cherry flavor. If you’ve been disappointed by bland or fake-tasting cherry recipes, this one’s about to change that.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Mixed Berry Ice Cream
ingredients: ¾ cup mixed berries (strawberries, blueberries, raspberries)
instructions: Add the half and half, cottage cheese, sweetened condensed milk, mixed berries, vanilla, and salt to a blender. Blend until completely smooth.
Pour the mixture into a Ninja Creami pint container, making sure not to go past the max fill line.
Cover with the lid and freeze on a level surface for 24 hours.
Remove the lid, place the pint in the outer bowl, secure the outer bowl lid, and lock it into the machine. Select Ice Cream.
If the texture looks dry or crumbly after spinning, add a splash of half and half or milk and run Respin.
Serve right away for a softer texture, or freeze for 20 to 30 minutes for firmer scoops.
recipes.prepNotes: Creamy, fruity, and easy to make, this Ninja Creami Mixed Berry Ice Cream blends strawberries, blueberries, and raspberries into one sweet-tart homemade ice cream with a smooth texture and real berry flavor.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Banana Ice Cream
ingredients: ¾ cup whole milk or 2% milk
instructions: Add the banana to your Ninja Creami pint container and mash with a fork until mostly smooth.
Pour in the sweetened condensed milk, milk, heavy cream, and vanilla extract. Stir until fully combined.
Freeze the pint on a flat surface for 24 hours.
Remove from freezer and let sit 1 to 2 minutes. Run warm water over the outside of the pint for 30 seconds.
Install pint in machine and run **Ice Cream** cycle.
If crumbly, add 1 tablespoon milk and run **Respin**.
Serve immediately for soft-serve texture or freeze 15 to 20 minutes for firmer scoops.
recipes.prepNotes: Creamy, real-banana Ninja Creami ice cream made with five simple ingredients and sweetened condensed milk for the smoothest texture. No icy pint here, just rich banana flavor and scoop-shop vibes straight from your freezer.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Strawberry Banana Ice Cream
instructions: Add the banana, strawberries, heavy cream, milk, sweetened condensed milk, vanilla extract, and salt to a blender.
Blend until completely smooth, about 30 to 45 seconds.
Strain the base through a fine mesh strainer if you want a smoother, seed-free texture.
Pour the mixture into a Ninja Creami pint container, staying at or below the max fill line.
Seal the pint and freeze on a level surface for at least 24 hours.
Remove the pint from the freezer and let it sit on the counter for 2 to 3 minutes.
Spin using the Ice Cream function on the Ninja Creami.
Respin once if the texture looks crumbly after the first spin.
recipes.prepNotes: This Ninja Creami strawberry banana ice cream is creamy, fruity, and naturally sweet with real strawberries and banana. It blends into a smooth pale pink base and spins into a soft, scoopable ice cream after freezing.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Strawberry Protein Frozen Yogurt
ingredients: 1 cup strawberries (hulled, fresh or frozen and thawed)
½ cup whole milk or 2% milk
¾ cup full-fat or 2% Greek yogurt
instructions: Add the cottage cheese, strawberries, Greek yogurt, allulose, vanilla extract, and milk to a blender. Blend until completely smooth, with no visible cottage cheese curds or strawberry pieces.
Pour the mixture into a Ninja Creami pint container, keeping it at or below the max fill line.
Seal the pint and freeze on a level surface for 24 hours.
Remove the pint from the freezer and let it sit at room temperature for 2 to 3 minutes.
Process on the Lite Ice Cream setting for a lighter froyo texture, or use Ice Cream for a denser result.
Respin once if the texture is crumbly after the first spin.
Scoop and serve immediately, or refreeze for 20 to 30 minutes for firmer scoops.
recipes.prepNotes: This Ninja Creami Strawberry Protein Frozen Yogurt is tangy, creamy, and packed with real strawberry flavor. Greek yogurt and cottage cheese add protein and body, while allulose keeps the texture smooth and scoopable.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami High Protein Strawberry Cheesecake Ice Cream
ingredients: Graham crackers (crushed or broken into small pieces)
instructions: Add the strawberries, cottage cheese, cream cheese, milk, allulose, and vanilla extract to a blender.
Blend until the mixture is completely smooth and creamy.
Pour the mixture into a Ninja Creami pint container, making sure it does not go past the max fill line.
Freeze on a level surface for 24 hours, or until completely solid.
Remove the pint from the freezer and place it into the Ninja Creami machine.
Spin on the  Ice Cream setting.
Respin if the texture looks crumbly or powdery. Add a small splash of milk before respinning if needed.
Add crushed graham crackers to the center of the pint.
Use the Mix-In setting to evenly incorporate the graham crackers.
Serve immediately and enjoy.
recipes.prepNotes: This Ninja Creami High Protein Strawberry Cheesecake Ice Cream is creamy, fruity, and tangy with real strawberries, cottage cheese, and cream cheese. Graham crackers mixed in at the end give it that classic strawberry cheesecake flavor.

recipes.title: Ninja Creami Olive Oil Ice Cream
ingredients: ½ cup sweetened condensed milk (or adjust with condensed milk)
2 teaspoon lemon juice  (or zest of 1 lemon)
instructions: Blend: Combine half and half, olive oil, sweetened condensed milk, vanilla, and lemon in a blender. Blend until smooth and fully emulsified — meaning the olive oil is completely mixed in with no oily layer on top. It should look creamy and uniform, not separated.
Freeze: Pour into a Ninja Creami pint and freeze for 24 hours on a level surface.
Spin: Run the “Ice Cream” mode on your Ninja Creami.
Respin: If the texture looks crumbly, make a small well in the center, add about 1 tablespoon of half and half, then respin until smooth.
Scoop & Serve: Scoop and enjoy right away, or drizzle extra olive oil and add mix-ins like sea salt or nuts.
recipes.prepNotes: This Ninja Creami olive oil ice cream is smooth, rich, and subtly fruity with a clean finish. Olive oil and lemon keep the texture creamy without feeling heavy.

recipes.title: Cinnamon Churro Ice Cream
instructions: Add all ingredients to a blender and blend until completely smooth.
Pour into a 24 oz Ninja Creami Deluxe pint. Freeze 24 hours.
Spin using the Ice Cream setting.
If crumbly, press Respin. Add 1–2 tbsp half &half only if needed.
For add-ins, create a well in the center and run Mix-In.
Scoop and enjoy, or freeze 15 minutes to firm up.
recipes.prepNotes:
* Use softened cream cheese — cold blocks won’t blend smoothly.
* For stronger churro flavor, add a pinch more cinnamon.
* If your pint freezes rock hard, give it 5 minutes on the counter before spinning.

recipes.title: Snickerdoodle Ice Cream
ingredients: 1 scoop of BUM Snickerdoodle Whey Protein
Optional: ¼ teaspoon of Cream of Tartar (adds tang)
instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Add mix-ins: Optional Snickerdoodle Cookie (Keto or store-bought), usually 1 serving per pint.
Top with Cool Whip and Cinnamon.
recipes.prepNotes: A soft, cinnamon-sugar coating with a slight bit of tang. Cream of Tartar adds the tang used in Snickerdoodle cookies but tastes fine without it. For a cheesecake version, see Cinnamon Cookie Crumble.

recipes.title: Lemon Bar Ice Cream
ingredients: 1 scoop of BUM Lemon Cake Whey Protein Powder OR Vanilla Ice Cream protein powder (but not as lemony)
¼ teaspoon of Cake Batter Extract
instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Optional mix-ins: For a more cake-like texture, use Bettergoods Lemon Chiffon Soft Baked Cookies or Lemon Bars (Keto/Regular or Quest Lemon Cake protein bars).
Make Lemon Icing: Mix 1 tablespoon of SF powdered sugar with 1/4 teaspoon of water and a couple drops of lemon juice/extract until an icing-like texture.
Drizzle icing over the top or mix with the Cool Whip first.
Optional: Top with Lemon Cookies or Lemon Bars.

recipes.title: Caramel Frapachino Ice Cream
ingredients: ¾ cup of Fairlife Skim Milk
¾ cup unsweetened vanilla almond milk
1 scoop Vanilla Ice Cream protein powder
2 Tbsp SF Caramel beverage syrup Torani
1 brewed shot of expresso/coffee (2 oz) OR 1 scoop of Nespresso (if using this option keep the Fairlife Milk at 1 Cup)
instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Spin up on lite ice cream setting for extra fluffy smoothness.
Top with Non Fat Cool Whip and SF Caramel Syrup.
recipes.prepNotes: Vanilla base with an expresso shot spun up on lite ice cream setting for extra fluffy smoothness and topped with cool whip and caramel syrup. No mix-ins.

recipes.title: Chocolate Fudge Almond Toffee Ice Cream
ingredients: ¾ cup unsweetened vanilla almond milk
1 scoop Chocolate Protein powder OR 50/50 mix with Ryse Cosmic Brownie
1 Tbsp of Dark Cocoa Powder OR Cocoa Powder
1 Tbsp PB2 Chocolate Chip Brownie Mix OR Brownie Mix
1/4 teaspoon of SF Skinny Girl Butter Toffee Syrup
instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Toast almonds in toaster oven (breakup first).
Add mix-ins: 14 grams toasted almonds, Heath Bar Toffee Bits, optional crumbled Keto brownie bits.
Top with Cool Whip, toffee bits, SF chocolate syrup, and brownie crumble.

recipes.title: Almond Fudge Ice Cream
ingredients: ¾ cup unsweetened vanilla almond milk
½ Tbsp of Dark Cocoa Powder (can use 1 Tbsp all dark if desired)
instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Toast almonds in toaster oven (careful not to burn!).
Add mix-ins: 14 grams toasted almonds, 17 grams (1 inch square) Chocolate Fudge.
Optional: Squirt SF Chocolate Syrup in the bottom of the pint.
Optional toppings: Non Fat Cool Whip and SF Chocolate Syrup.

recipes.title: Toasted Coconut Almond Ice Cream
ingredients: ¾ cup unsweetened vanilla almond milk
instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Toast almonds in toaster oven, then crush (put in ziplock bag and crush with spoon).
Toast 2 Tbsp coconut flakes in toaster oven until slightly brown.
Add mix-ins: 14 grams toasted crushed almonds, 2 Tbsp toasted coconut flakes, Lilies Dark Chocolate Chips OR Regular Chocolate Chips, optional Almond Joy.
Sprinkle a bit of toasted almonds and toasted coconut flakes on top. Optional: Non Fat Cool Whip.

recipes.title: Dulce De Leche Ice Cream
ingredients: ¾ cup unsweetened vanilla almond milk
1 scoop Vanilla Ice Cream protein powder
2 Tbsp Davinci SF Dulche De Leche Syrup AND/OR 100 grams Dulche de Leche Pudding (wet)
instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
No mix-ins.
Top with Non Fat Cool Whip (Optional: mix in bowl with 1 Tbsp of Dulche De Leche Syrup before adding) and SF Caramel Sauce.

recipes.title: Cookies Over Texas Ice Cream
ingredients: ¾ cup unsweetened vanilla almond milk
instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Add mix-ins: Chocolate chip Cookie Dough (Keto or Regular), Oreo's (SF Oreos or Oreo Thins/Minis/Regular, can remove the cream for fewer calories).
Optional toppings: Cool Whip, SF Chocolate Syrup, Chocolate Sprinkles, crumbled cookie bits.

recipes.title: Banana Nut Ice Cream - untested
ingredients: ¾ cup unsweetened vanilla almond milk
instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Add mix-ins: Crushed Walnuts OR Pecans, optional Keto Banana Nut Bread/Muffins.
Optional toppings: Crushed Walnuts OR Pecans, Cool Whip, sliced banana.
recipes.prepNotes: Untested. Straight from grandma's kitchen. A Banana cream base with smashed banana and mixed with earthy chopped walnuts. Similar to banana pudding but with nuts for crunch and banana bread.

recipes.title: Cosmic Brownie Ice Cream
ingredients: 1 tbsp sugar-free Chocolate OR White Chocolate pudding
1 scoop of Ryse Cosmic Brownie Protein Powder OR Optium Nutrition Extreme Chocolate Protein Powder (won't be as brownie flavored)
1 Tbsp Dark Cocoa Powder OR Cocoa Powder OR Half Tbsp of each as desired
Bulk Option: add 1 Tbsp of PB2 Chocolate Chip Brownie Mix OR Equivalent
Lower Calorie Non-Mixin Optional: Swap Little Debbie Cosmic Brownie with 1 Tbsp of Pillsbury Zero Sugar Brownie Mix OR Make Keto Fudge Brownie
Mix-ins: 1 Little Debbie's Cosmic Brownie diced up OR Keto Fudge Brownie
Toppings: Sugar Free Hershey Chocolate Syrup
instructions: Combine all base ingredients and mix well.
Churn as a traditional dark chocolate ice cream.
Add the diced Cosmic Brownie and rainbow sprinkles during the mix-in cycle.
Top with rainbow sprinkles and sugar free Hershey chocolate syrup.

recipes.title: Oatmeal Cream Pie Ice Cream
ingredients: 1 scoop of Ryse Jet Puff Marshmallow OR 1 Keto Iced Oatmeal Whey Protein Powder
Optional: Reduce each milk by 1/4 cup and replace with 1/4 cup of cooked oatmeal – rolled oats and water (Optional add cinnamon and honey)
Mix-ins: 1 Little Debbie's Oatmeal Cream Pie, broken up into smaller pieces
Cream Filling Topping: 1 tbsp room temp Butter, 15 gr Serve Powdered Sugar, 1/8 teaspoon vanilla extract, 4 ml heavy cream
instructions: Combine all base ingredients and mix well.
Churn as a traditional vanilla ice cream.
Add broken up Oatmeal Cream Pie pieces during the mix-in cycle.

For cream filling topping: beat 1 tbsp room temp butter until fluffy (a few minutes), then add 15 gr powdered sugar and mix well, then add 1/8 teaspoon vanilla extract and 4 ml heavy cream and mix well. Add more heavy cream as needed until desired texture (like icing).

recipes.title: Star Crunch Ice Cream
ingredients: Mix-ins: 1 Frozen Little Debbie's Star Crunch OR Optional healthier Alternative: Rice Crispies soaked with SF Chocolate Syrup hand stirred in
Toppings: Optional SF Chocolate AND/OR SF Caramel Syrup
instructions: Combine all base ingredients and mix well.
Churn as a traditional vanilla ice cream.
Add the frozen Star Crunch (or soaked rice crispies) during the mix-in cycle.
Top with optional SF chocolate and/or SF caramel syrup.
recipes.prepNotes: A traditionally vanilla ice cream with hints of caramel and chocolate-covered rice crisp. Edit: Still Trying to refine as of 3/17/26.

recipes.title: Red Velvet Ice Cream
ingredients: 1 tbsp sugar-free Red Velvet Cupcake pudding (Sprinkles brand)
Optional: 2-4 drops of red food coloring (Just darkens the mix)
instructions: Combine all base ingredients and mix well.
Churn as a traditional vanilla ice cream.
Add cream cheese during the mix-in cycle: pre-freeze it in small clumps for chunks, or leave at fridge temperature for a more dispersed icing-like flavor through the pint.
recipes.prepNotes: A traditional vanilla ice cream complemented with Red Velvet Pudding and cream cheese for that signature red velvet taste. Cream cheese can dial up the fat, so opt for Low Fat or Non Fat Cream Cheese if watching macros.

recipes.title: Cinnamon Toast Crunch Ice Cream
ingredients: 1 scoop of Ryse Cinnamon Toast Crunch Protein Powder
Mix-ins: 1/2 cup of Cinnamon Toast Crunch Cereal, OR Catalina Crunch Protein Cereal, adjust as desired
instructions: Combine all base ingredients and mix well.
Churn as a traditional vanilla ice cream.
Add cinnamon and cereal during the mix-in cycle.
Top with Cool Whip, Cinnamon Toast Crunch Cereal, and a dash of cinnamon.

recipes.title: Sherbert (Lime, Orange, Raspberry, Strawberry)
ingredients: 1 tbsp sugar-free JELLO (Stir/Whisk in 1/2 cup hot water until it dissolves or it will have a grainy texture)
1/4 teaspoon Flavored Extract of desired flavor (Lime, Lemon, Strawberry, ...)

recipes.title: גלידת בייגלה מלוח 🍦🥨
ingredients: לרוטב בייגלה מלוח (שהוא חלק מבסיס הגלידה, לא רוטב להגשה):
1/2 כוס בייגלה (השתמשתי בשמיניות ללא מלח - בערך 25-30 גרם)
גביע (200 גרם) יוגורט חלבון בטעם קרמל מלוח או טבעי
סקופ (30 גרם) אבקת חלבון בטעם וניל / קרמל מלוח (או 2 כפות אינסטנט פודינג)
instructions: מכינים את רוטב הבייגלה המלוח.
מפזרים את תחליף הסוכר על מחבת ומחממים על אש בינונית עד שהוא הופך נוזלי/מתקרמל.
מוסיפים את הבייגלה, המלח החלב והשמנת. מערבבים תוך כדי, מבשלים כ-4-5 דקות ומכבים את האש. מסננים את הרוטב מהבייגלה ומניחים בצד.
במיכל מערבבים את מצרכי הגלידה (השתמשתי ביוגורט טבעי) אפשר גם בקרמל מלוח. ובסוף מוסיפים את הרוטב שהכנו.
מכסים את המיכל ומקפיאים ל-24 שעות.
מפעילים את המכונה, מכניסים לערבול "גלידה לייט".
יוצרים חור במרכז, מוסיפים בייגלה (אופציונלי) ומכניסים לערבול "תוספות".

1 tbsp sugar free chocolate caramel coffee syrup
instructions: Mix together all ingredients except Snickers
Freeze 24 hours.
Spin on lite ice cream. Add 1 tbsp. milk and respin 1-2x.
Add in 1/2 chopped snickers bar.
Spin on mixin.

recipes.title: Blueberry Vanilla Ice Cream
ingredients: 1/2 Tb sugar free vanilla jello pudding mix
instructions: Add milk, protein powder, and jello mix to container and blend until smooth.
Add blueberries and stir with spoon.
Put lid on and freeze for 12-24 hours.
I blend mine once using light ice cream and one more time using the mix in setting!

recipes.title: Oreo McFlurry Ice Cream
instructions: Blend all ingredients for the base and freeze (at least 16 hours)
Run your pint under hot water for at least 60 seconds
Spin on "Lite Ice Cream" setting
Spin on "Respin" setting if very powdery
Make a hole down to the bottom with a butter knife and fill with the Oreos
Run the "Mix-in" cycle

recipes.title: Ben and Jerry's Cake Batter Ice Cream
ingredients: 40-45g Lakanto Classic White Monk Fruit sweetener
15g chocolate chips mixed with 2g of coconut oil (mix-in)
instructions: Blend all ingredients for the base and freeze (at least 16 hours)
Run your pint under hot water for at least 60 seconds
Spin on "Ice Cream" setting
Spin on "Respin" setting if very powdery
Make a hole down to the bottom with a butter knife and fill with the melted chocolate
Run the "Mix-in" cycle
Top with the chocolate frosting
recipes.prepNotes: Right before the Mix-In cycle, combine the chocolate with the coconut oil, melt in the microwave (30 sec intervals, stirring in between), hollow out a hole in the center of the base, and pour in. After spinning on the Mix-In setting, it'll turn into perfect, creamy chocolate chips. To make the chocolate frosting, combine 15g of unsweetened cocoa powder with 30g of sugar free syrup (I know this sounds like it wouldn't work, but trust me... it does).

recipes.title: Pistachio Ice Cream
instructions: Combine the ingredients for the ice cream in a blender until smooth. Transfer into the tub and freeze overnight.
Mix the on the Lite Ice Cream setting.
Respin as many times as needed until you reach the desired consistency, adding in 1 tbsp milk before each respin.
Add in the pistachios and hit Mix In.

recipes.title: **Vanilla Bean FroYo
instructions: Blend all ingredients for the base and freeze (at least 16 hours)
Run your pint under hot water for at least 60 seconds
Spin on "Lite Ice Cream" setting
Spin on "Respin" setting until it reaches your desired texture

recipes.title: Chocolate Ice Cream 2
ingredients: 1 tsp date paste or honey (to taste)

recipes.title: Barebells Ice Cream
instructions: Blend all ingredients for the base and freeze (at least 16 hours)
Run your pint under hot water for at least 60 seconds
Spin on "Lite Ice Cream" setting
Spin on "Respin" setting if very powdery
Right before the Mix-In cycle, microwave the Barebells bar for 10-20 seconds, hollow out a hole in the center of the base, and dump in.
Make a hole down to the bottom with a butter knife and fill with the melty Barebells
Run the "Mix-in" cycle

recipes.title: Cafe Latte
instructions: Mix all ingredients and pour into tub
Freeze x 24 hrs
Spin on light ice cream setting. Add 2 tbsp vanilla shake and re-spin

recipes.title: Frozen Yoghurt
ingredients: 25–50 g light agave syrup or pure maple syrup
instructions: Whisk all ingredients together (mixing in a separate bowl helps remove air lumps), then pour into the tub.
Freeze 16–24 hours with the lid off.
Let sit on the bench 5–15 minutes before processing.
Process on the light ice cream function — generally needs two spins.
Store leftovers in the freezer with the lid on.
recipes.prepNotes: Use a yoghurt that's already sweetened. Approx. 48g protein with Danone high protein vanilla yoghurt + high protein skim milk.

recipes.title: Cookie Dough Ice Cream
ingredients: 150 g sweetened condensed milk (not evaporated milk)
1 tsp vanilla bean paste or extract
20–40 ml + 1–2 tbsp milk of choice, as needed
1 tsp vanilla bean paste or extract
instructions: Vanilla ice cream: Whisk cream, condensed milk, milk, vanilla together in a bowl, let sit 5 minutes, whisk again.
Pour into a tub and freeze with the lid off, 16–24 hours.
Cookie dough (30–60 min before processing): Brown the butter in a saucepan over low-medium heat until golden and nutty-smelling.
Pour over brown sugar in a heatproof bowl; cool 5 minutes.
Add 1 tbsp milk and stir to a syrupy brown liquid.
Add tapioca flour in batches until it forms a non-oily, rollable dough (add more milk if it crumbles).
Cool, then stir in chopped chocolate. Roll into balls or leave as crumbles; chill at least 10 minutes.
To assemble: Process the frozen ice cream base on the ice cream function (re-spin if balled up).
Fold chilled cookie dough into the spun ice cream in a large bowl.
Pack back into the tub; freeze ~20 minutes until firm. Serve.
recipes.prepNotes: Cookie dough should be prepared 30–60 minutes before processing the ice cream base.

recipes.title: Maple Pumpkin Ice Cream
instructions: Whisk ingredients together in a bowl; taste and adjust maple syrup to taste.
Pour into the tub and freeze with the lid off, 16–24 hours.
Process on the ice cream function; re-spin if needed.
Return leftovers to the freezer with the lid on.
recipes.prepNotes: Homemade pumpkin puree is sweeter than canned, so needs less maple syrup. Stick to 75–100g maple syrup for proper texture.

recipes.title: Sticky Date Ice Cream
ingredients: 100 g regular pitted dates, finely chopped
1/2–1 tsp vanilla bean extract, to taste
30 g whey protein isolate with minimal sweetener
instructions: Place chopped dates in a heatproof bowl; sprinkle with baking soda and pour over boiling water. Stir and cool.
Transfer cooled date paste to a food processor with milk, salt, and vanilla; blend to desired consistency.
Return mixture to the bowl and whisk in the whey protein isolate (do not blend the protein — it gets too frothy).
Pour into the tub and freeze with the lid off, 16–24 hours.
Process on the light ice cream function; re-spin if needed. Serve immediately; return leftovers to the freezer with the lid on.
recipes.prepNotes: 3g baking soda for subtle flavour, 5g for a classic sticky date note. Do not substitute baking powder. Avoid high-sugar protein powders — too much sugar will prevent proper freezing.

recipes.title: Watermelon Sorbet
ingredients: 400 g watermelon, chopped into small cubes
25 g lime juice (or more, to taste)
instructions: Blend all ingredients (and optional add-ins) until completely smooth.
Pour into the tub (don't exceed fill line) and freeze with the lid off, 16–24 hours.
Process on the gelato function; re-spin once or twice as needed.
Return leftovers to the freezer with the lid on; re-spin on the gelato function before eating.

recipes.title: Coffee Ice Cream
ingredients: 50 g fresh espresso, cooled for 5 minutes
100 g full fat milk (regular or lactose free)
instructions: Whisk all ingredients together in a bowl until completely smooth.
Pour into the tub; optionally chill in the fridge 3 hours to minimize the hump, then freeze with the lid off, 16–24 hours.
Process on the ice cream function; smooth a balled-up top with a spoon, or re-spin once if powdery (avoid over-processing — it can split the cream).

recipes.title: Coconut Ice Cream
ingredients: 175–200 g full fat or light coconut milk
Dash of vanilla bean paste or extract (optional)
instructions: Whisk all ingredients together; taste and adjust salt/vanilla.
Pour into the tub and freeze with the lid off, 12–24 hours.
Let thaw 10 minutes before processing (optional, reduces need to re-spin).
Process on the ice cream function; re-process (no added liquid) if powdery or balled up.
Store leftovers in the freezer with the lid on.
recipes.prepNotes: Choose coconut cream with ~20–25g fat per 100g.

recipes.title: Custard Ice Cream
ingredients: 3 extra large eggs (150g out of shell)
instructions: Whisk eggs, sugar, and cream together in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat milk, vanilla, and salt in a saucepan until hot and just below a simmer.
Slowly pour the hot milk into the egg mixture while whisking.
Pour the combined mixture back into the saucepan and cook over low/medium heat, stirring constantly, until it thickens slightly (about 70°C, ~5 minutes).
If it starts to clump, remove from heat immediately and whisk vigorously; transfer out of the pot to stop cooking.
Cool slightly, then transfer to the tub and freeze 16–24 hours with the lid off.
Spin on the ice cream function — needs two full spins to be perfect.
recipes.prepNotes: 4 eggs can be used for higher protein. Lower sugar to 50g if mixture is too soft.

recipes.title: Strawberry Ricotta Ice Cream
instructions: Blend strawberries to a smooth puree. Add remaining ingredients and pulse to combine until smooth and light pink.
Pour into the tub and freeze 16–24 hours with the lid off.
Process on the ice cream function — needs 3 spins for ideal texture.

recipes.title: Chocolate Caramel Ice Cream
instructions: Whisk cocoa with boiling water in a saucepan to remove clumps.
Add remaining ingredients and whisk to combine; crush any caramel chunks.
Cook on low heat 2–5 minutes until caramel is dissolved. Cool slightly.
Pour into the tub and freeze 16–24 hours with the lid off.
Process on the ice cream function — may need two spins.
Mix in chopped chocolate caramel eggs by hand (not via the mix-in function).
Return leftovers to the freezer with the lid on.

recipes.title: Sugar Free Strawberry Ice Cream
ingredients: 300 g fresh strawberries, weighed after hulling
Optional: Few cracks freshly cracked black pepper
instructions: Hull, chop, and mash strawberries in a pot to release liquid.
Add allulose and stir to combine.
Cook over medium heat 2–3 minutes until allulose dissolves and mixture is a vibrant red coulis. Stir in any flavour boosters, then cool 15 minutes.
Transfer cooled mixture to the tub.
Add cream and mix thoroughly.
Freeze with the lid off, 16–24 hours.
Process on the ice cream function — should be perfect on first spin with 100–125g allulose.
Return leftovers to the fridge with the lid on.
recipes.prepNotes: Do not exceed 125g allulose — the mixture won't set. Less sweet strawberries may need more allulose; very sweet ones may need only 75–100g.

recipes.title: Sugar Free Lemon Sorbet
instructions: Mix allulose and stevia in a saucepan; rub in lemon zest.
Add water and salt, then lemon juice to taste.
Cook 1–3 minutes until allulose dissolves. Cool slightly.
Pour into the tub and freeze with the lid off, 24 hours.
Process on the sorbet function; re-spin once if needed (avoid spinning twice — can make it too soft).
Return leftovers to the freezer with the lid on; re-process before eating.
recipes.prepNotes: Use common sour lemons rather than Meyer/Lemonade lemons for the classic tart flavour.

recipes.title: Healthier Orange Creamsicle Protein Ice Cream
instructions: Combine ingredients and freeze 12–24 hours.
Spin on the Lite Ice Cream setting.
Add 1 tbsp milk and re-spin 1–2x until smooth.
recipes.prepNotes: tastes like a copycat DQ Orange Julius

recipes.title: **Apple Butter Biscoff Protein Ice Cream
ingredients: 1–2 tbsp apple butter or apple pie filling
Pinch of cinnamon/nutmeg or apple pie spice (not too much!)
instructions: Mix base ingredients together in a Ninja Creami pint and freeze 24 hours.
Once frozen, spin on the Lite Ice Cream setting.
Respin if needed.
Add in apple butter and Biscoff cookies as mix-ins and spin on respin.

recipes.title: Sweet Potato Ice Cream
ingredients: 150 g sweetened condensed milk (not evaporated milk)
instructions: Whisk all ingredients together until smooth and spices are integrated.
Pour into the tub and freeze 16–24 hours with the lid off.
Process on the ice cream function. Re-spin for a fluffier texture if desired.
recipes.prepNotes: Sweet potato must be 100% puree, no added sugar/syrup. Canned sweet potato not tested.

recipes.title: Classic Ice Cream Base Recipe
ingredients: 1 tbsp cream cheese (optional — makes the ice cream thicker)
instructions: Mix all ingredients together in a pint tub and freeze 24 hours
Once frozen, spin on the ice cream setting.
The ice cream will come out slightly crumbly — add 1 tbsp milk and respin.
recipes.prepNotes: For a thicker, still-creamy texture, freeze an additional 1–3 hours.

instructions: mix all and pour in tub

recipes.title: Chocolate Ice Cream 1
instructions: Heat milk and sugar to melt
Add cream and vanilla
Pour into tub
